mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 40% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, or the Rank IC is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and economic contraction, which typically precedes or accompanies declines in corporate profits and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of unemployment spikes and recessions
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the UNRATE increase does not correlate with subsequent SPX decline.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) show signs of stabilization or recovery while the Federal Reserve maintains an accommodative monetary policy (low FEDFUNDS), it indicates improving consumer demand supported by easy credit, which is bullish for equities over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth turns positive AND FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where both conditions are met, or the interaction term is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and contracting GDP (GDPC1) indicates stagflationary pressures, which erode corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the combined effect of inflation and negative GDP growth does not predict negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) below critical levels signals a severe contraction in the construction sector and broader economic weakness, leading to reduced investment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500 thousand units (3-month average)
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or HOUST below 500k units does not precede negative SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing and mining activity, indicating strong economic fundamentals that support corporate earnings and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 40% of periods where INDPRO growth is positive, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX returns is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is at or near the zero lower bound, it indicates an extremely accommodative monetary policy aimed at stimulating the economy, which typically supports asset prices and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS being at the zero lower bound.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals an improving labor market and economic recovery, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:early signs of recovery after recession
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or UNRATE decline does not correlate with subsequent positive SPX returns.
